behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this checks handoff across parser + hook-input handling, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real subprocess/network/OS. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: no agent pipeline. Rejected System because text only says 'proceed through normal hook input handling' and does not require real process or external round-trip artifact.
behaviors.test_description: Run an in-process test wiring real parser + hook input handler with representative markdown-heavy JSON strings (fences, backticks, $vars) and assert successful parse plus downstream handled state.

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears when parse error object is transformed into trace payload across modules. REAL-INFRA: no explicit requirement for real subprocess or external service contract. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: no. Rejected System because behavior does not require real process execution or external round-trip readback, only that trace record is produced with diagnostic context.
behaviors.test_description: In-process pipeline test: feed malformed JSON into parser+trace-mapper, assert `json_parse_failed` record exists and includes expected context fields (location/snippet/reason) for diagnosis.

behaviors.description: The local error-policy path maps parse failure to a handled result (no throw) and marks telemetry intent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: mapping parse failure -> policy result + telemetry intent is a module interaction path. REAL-INFRA: explicitly 'local error-policy path', so no real OS/network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected Unit because unit-only can miss integration of real parse-error shape into policy mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Drive a real parse failure through parser output into the error-policy component and assert no exception, handled result returned, and telemetry-intent flag set.

behaviors.description: In real process execution, malformed stdin does not crash the hook process and still emits the expected trace signal before exit.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA + SYSTEM TRIAD are explicit: 'real process execution' with malformed stdin, process survival/exit behavior, and externally visible trace emission before exit. In-process fakes can hide subprocess/stdio timing and exit-code behavior. No LLM dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Spawn the real hook process, send malformed bytes on stdin, assert process exits without crash semantics, and verify expected trace signal is emitted before termination.

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA + ROUND-TRIP EFFECT RULE: behavior requires a real invocation path and proving an external trace artifact via stdin->parser->sink round trip. This is explicitly beyond in-process module wiring. No LLM dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the hook through its real entrypoint with malformed stdin and assert the trace artifact is present in the real sink/output format with the expected parse-failure signal.

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: discrimination requires running both payload classes through shared parsing/error-classification flow across modules. REAL-INFRA: no explicit real subprocess/network requirement in behavior text. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected System because text does not require real process path or external side-effect readback, only correct classification/regression distinction.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven in-process regression over two classes (valid markdown-heavy JSON vs malformed JSON) through parser+classification/trace mapping, asserting only malformed inputs yield parse-failure classification.
